description: The Taliban is an Afghan political and militant organization that emerged in 1994 during the Second Afghan Civil War. Founded by Mullah Omar, it ruled most of Afghanistan from 1996 to 2001, enforcing a strict interpretation of sharia law. Overthrown by a US-led invasion in 2001, it waged a 20-year insurgency before recapturing Kabul in 2021 and now controls all of Afghanistan. The group is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries and is widely condemned for human rights abuses.

country_links.evidence_quote: In October 2025, India affirmed its support for Afghanistan and upgraded its Kabul technical mission to embassy status.
country_links.context: India upgraded its Kabul technical mission to embassy status and affirmed support for Afghanistan.

country_links.context: The Taliban attempted to improve relations with the Shia-led Government of Iran.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 23 January 2022, a Taliban delegation arrived in Oslo, and closed-door meetings were held during the Taliban's first official trip to Western Europe...
country_links.context: The Taliban delegation held talks in Oslo with Western diplomats.

country_links.evidence_quote: Others were taken to Khost, where bin Laden had several training camps... the trail of many of the vanished women leads to Pakistan where they were sold to brothels or into private households to be kept as slaves.
country_links.context: The Taliban's human trafficking network sold abducted women into sex slavery in Pakistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: former head of the group's political office in Doha
country_links.context: The Taliban maintained a political office in Doha, Qatar, for negotiations.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 10 October 2021, Russia hosted the Taliban for talks in Moscow in an effort to boost its influence across Central Asia.
country_links.context: Russia hosted the Taliban for talks in Moscow and later recognized the Taliban as the de jure government of Afghanistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: the US CIA and the Saudi Arabian General Intelligence Directorate (GID) funnelled funding and equipment through the Pakistani Inter-Service Intelligence Agency (ISI) to the Afghan mujahideen.
country_links.context: Saudi Arabia provided support to the Taliban during the Soviet-Afghan War.

country_links.evidence_quote: gained diplomatic recognition from only three states: the United Arab Emirates, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia
country_links.context: The Taliban regime gained diplomatic recognition from the United Arab Emirates during its first rule.
